label: 3356 Fast-tracking sales to Taiwan under foreign military sales program

children.heading: Preclearance of certain foreign military sales items
children.label: a Preclearance of certain foreign military sales items

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense, and in conjunction with coordinating entities such as the National Disclosure Policy Committee, the Arms Transfer and Technology Release Senior Steering Group, and other appropriate entities, shall compile a list of available and emerging military platforms, technologies, and equipment that are pre-cleared and prioritized for sale and release to Taiwan through the Foreign Military Sales program.December 23, 2022

children.heading: Selection of items
children.label: A Selection of items
children.text: The list compiled pursuant to paragraph (1) shall not be construed as limiting the type, timing, or quantity of items that may be requested by, or sold to, Taiwan under the Foreign Military Sales program.

children.heading: Notifications required
children.label: B Notifications required
children.text: Nothing in this chapter may be construed to supersede congressional notification requirements under the Arms Export Control Act ( et. seq.).22 U.S.C. 2751

children.heading: Prioritized processing of foreign military sales requests from Taiwan
children.label: b Prioritized processing of foreign military sales requests from Taiwan

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 1 Requirement
children.text: The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Defense shall prioritize and expedite the processing of requests from Taiwan under the Foreign Military Sales program, and may not delay the processing of requests for bundling purposes.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 2 Duration
children.text: The requirement under paragraph (1) shall continue until the Secretary of State determines and certifies to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives that the threat to Taiwan has significantly abated.

children.heading: Interagency policy
children.label: c Interagency policy
children.text: The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Defense shall jointly review and update interagency policies and implementation guidance related to Foreign Military Sales requests from Taiwan, including incorporating the preclearance provisions of this section.
